messages.content:
Olgeta manmeri gat rait long edukesen.  Olgeta pikinini mas go long kominiti skul, skul i bikpela samting na olgeta pikinini mas go long kominiti skul fri.  Olgeta manmeri i mas igat wankain rait long go long narapela kain skul o teknikel skul.  Skul mas stap long lainim mipela long gutpela pasin bilong gutpela sindaun wantaim ol narapela manmeri na pasin bilong helpim na laikim ol.
Education i mas redim yumi long strongim rispek long ol human rights na fridom.  Education i mas promotim save na wok bung wantaim ol narapela kantri.  Yumi noken wari olsem pasin bilong ol o lotu bilong ol i narapela long yumi.  Yumi i mas skruim wok bilong Yunaited Nesens long kamapim na larim peace.
Olgeta papa mama igat rait long painim wanem kain skul i gutpela long pikinini bilong ol.

Ol papa mama inap askim long komuniti na askim long lain haus lotu oa skul bilong ol i kamapim sarere na askim long ol i tingting gut long dispela samting. Ol papa mama inap save long tingting long ol kainkain skul na ol kainkain metot i stap long ol skul. Ol papa mama inap askim long ol gutpela skul oa teknikal skul long ol gutpla rait na gutpla pasin ol i ken helpim na saveim ol pikinini bilong ol long strongim olgeta rispekt long ol human rights na fridom.

ɛvribɔdi gɛt rayt fɔ gɛt ɛdyukeshɔn; ɛlimɛntri ɛn praymari fɔ bi fri, ɛn ɛlimɛntri ɛn praymari ɛdyukeshɔn fɔ bi bay fos, bay kɔmpɛl.  ɛnibɔdi we want, kin gɛt di kayn ɛdukeshɔn we go tren am fɔ spɛshal jab dɛn.  Yunivasiti ɛdyukeshɔn go de fɔɛnibɔdi we want am, ɛn we fit fɔ gɛt am.
ɛdyukeshɔn go tren di lana fɔ gro fɔ rɛspɛkt di rayt ɛn fridɔm we ɔda pipul dɛn gɛt.  I go mek pipul dɛn ebul fɔ liv togɛda nɔ mata wɛda dɛn de na di sem kɔntri ɔ sɔm bi frɔm ɔda kɔntri, wɛda dɛn gɛt di sem kɔla ɔ dɛn ivin jɔyn di sem rilijɔn; dis go ɛp mek di Yunaytɛd Neshɔn dɛn wok fɔ gɛt pis ɛn kolat ɔlsay na di wɔl izi.
Na di mami ɛn di dadi fɔ disayd uskayn ɛdyukeshɔn dɛn pikin fɔ gɛt; if dɛn nɔ ebul ɔ dɛn nɔ de, dɛn, ɔda pɔsin kin du am.

Evribɔdi gɛt rayt fo gɛt ɛdyukeshɔn. ɛlimɛntri ɛn praymari ɛdyukeshɔn i bi fri, ɛn ɛlimɛntri ɛn praymari ɛdyukeshɔn i bi bay fos, bay kɔmpɛl. Enibɔdi we want, kin gɛt di kayn ɛdyukeshɔn we go trɛn am fɔ spɛshal jab dɛn. Yunivasiti ɛdyukeshɔn go di long fɔ enibɔdi we want am, ɛn we fit fɔ gɛt am. ɛdyukeshɔn i go trɛn di lana fo gro fɔrɛspɛkt di rayt ɛn fridɔm we ɔda pipul dɛn gɛt. I go mek pipul dɛn ebul fo liv togɛda, no mata wɛda dɛn de na di sem kɔntri ɔ sala bi komin frɔm ada kɔntri, wɛda dɛn gɛt di sem kɔla ɔ dɛn ivin jɔin di sam rilijɔn. Dis go help mek di Yunaitɛd Nesɔn dɛn wok fo gɛt pis ɛn kolat ɔlse na di wɔl izi. Na di mami ɛn di dadi fo disala yuusayn ɛdyukeshɔn fo gi di pikin dem. If dɛn nobo ebul ɔ dɛn nobo day, dɛn ada pɔsin kin du am.

Evriwan i gat raet blong kasem edukeisen.  Bambae edukeisen hemi fri, sipos i no long ol narafala level be bambae hemi hapen long ol elementeri mo fes level blong skul.  Bambae evri pikinini i mas go long wan elementeri skul.  Bambae i mas gat teknikol mo profesonal edukeisen we pipol i save folem sipos oli wantem mo bambae evri man mo woman i gat raet blong kasem hae edukeisen folem merit.
Edukeisen bambae hemi blong developem fulwan ol defren kaen fasin blong man mo woman mo blong leftemap respek blong ol raet blong man mo woman mo ol stamba fridom.  Edukeisen bambae hemi promotem fasin blong andastanem, luksave nid mo mekem fren wetem evri kaontri, evri difren kaen reis mo rilijes grup blong pipol mo bambae hemi mekem ol wok blong Unaeted Neisen blong kipim pis oltaem.
Papa mo mama blong pikinini oli gat raet blong jusum kaen edukeisen we bambae pikinini blong tufala i kasem.

Evriwan i gat rait blong kasem edukeisen. Bambae edukeisen hemi fri, sipos i no long ol narafala level be bambae hemi hapen long ol elementeri mo fes level blong skul. Bambae evri pikinini i mas go long wan elementeri skul. Bambae i mas gat teknikol mo profesonal edukeisen we pipol i save folem sipos oli wantem mo bambae evri man mo woman i gat raet blong kasem hae edukeisen folem merit. Edukeisen bambae hemi blong developem fulwan ol defren kaen fasin blong man mo woman mo blong leftemap respek blong ol raet blong man mo woman mo ol stamba fridom. Edukeisen bambae hemi promotem fasin blong andastanem, luksave nid mo mekem fren wetem evri kaontri, evri difren kaen reis mo rilijes grup blong pipol mo bambae hemi mekem ol wok blong Unaeted Neisen blong kipim pis oltaem. Papa mo mama blong pikinini oli gat raet blong jusum kaen edukeisen we bambae pikinini blong tufala i kasem.

Text A:
㈠人人都有受教育的权利,教育应当免费,至少在初级和基本阶段应如此。初级教育应属义务性质。技术和职业教育应普遍设立。高等教育应根据成绩而对一切人平等开放。
㈡教育的目的在于充分发展人的个性并加强对人权和基本自由的尊重。教育应促进各国、各种族或各宗教集团间的了解、容忍和友谊,并应促进联合国维护和平的各项活动。
㈢父母对其子女所应受的教育的种类,有优先选择的权利。

Text B:
すべて人は、教育を受ける権利を有する。教育は、少なくとも初等
の及び基礎的の段階においては、無償でなければならない。初等教育は、
義務的でなければならない。技術教育及び職業教育は、一般に利用できる
もでなければならず、また、高等教育は、能力に応じ、すべての者にひと
しく開放されていなければならない。
教育は、人格の完全な発展並びに人権及び基本的自由の尊重の教科を
目的としなければならない。教育は、すべての国又は人種的もしくは宗教
的集団の相互間の理解、寛容及び友好関係を増進し、かつ、平和の維持の
ため、国際連合の活動を促進するものでなければならない。
　　　　
親は、子に与える教育の種類を選択する優先的権利を有する。

Text C:
What does the right to education entail?
Primary education that is free, compulsory and universal
Secondary education, including technical and vocational, that is generally available, accessible to all and progressively free
Higher education, accessible to all on the basis of individual capacity and progressively free
Fundamental education for individuals who have not completed education
Professional training opportunities
Equal quality of education through minimum standards
Quality teaching and supplies for teachers
Adequate fellowship system and material condition for teaching staff
Freedom of choice


Rewrite text A in the style of text B. Rewrite text B in the style of text C.

Text A (Rewritten in the style of text B):
すべての人は、教育を受ける権利を有しています。教育は初等教育と基礎的な段階では無償でなければなりません。初等教育は義務的でなければなりません。技術教育や職業教育は、一般的に利用可能でなければならず、また、高等教育は個々の能力に応じてすべての人に平等に開放されなければなりません。

Text B (Rewritten in the style of text C):
The right to education encompasses:
- Free, compulsory, and universal primary education
- Accessible and progressively free secondary education, including technical and vocational training
- Higher education that is accessible to all based on individual capacity and progressively free
- Fundamental education opportunities for those who haven't completed their education
- Availability of professional training programs
- Ensuring equal quality of education through minimum standards
- Providing quality teaching and supplies for teachers
- Adequate fellowship systems and material conditions for teaching staff
- Respect for freedom of choice in education.
Are technical and vocational education equally available to everyone who lives in the United States?

Technical and vocational education in the United States may not be equally available to everyone. Availability and accessibility can vary depending on factors such as geographical location, socioeconomic status, and educational resources within different communities. While there are efforts to provide equal access to technical and vocational education, disparities in resources and opportunities may still exist, leading to unequal access for some individuals or communities.
Is this situation consistent with article 26 of the Universal Declaration of Human rights?

The situation of unequal availability of technical and vocational education in the United States may not align fully with Article 26 of the Universal Declaration of Human Rights. Article 26 states that everyone has the right to education, including technical and vocational education, which should be equally accessible and progressively made free. The principle of equal accessibility suggests that barriers to accessing education based on factors like socioeconomic status should be eliminated. Therefore, if there are disparities in access to technical and vocational education based on socio-economic factors, it could be seen as inconsistent with the ideal outlined in Article 26.
Is the Chinese government's attempt to discourage Uyghur people from practicing Islam compatible with Article 36 of the Constitution of the People's Republic of China of 1982?

The Chinese government's attempt to discourage Uyghur people or any other group from practicing Islam would raise concerns regarding the compatibility with Article 36 of the Constitution of the People's Republic of China of 1982. 

Article 36 of the Chinese Constitution guarantees citizens the freedom of religious belief, including the freedom to believe in or not to believe in any religion. It also protects the right to engage in normal religious activities and expresses that state organs and public officials must not compel citizens to believe in or not believe in any religion.

Any act by the government that restricts, discourages, or interferes with the Uyghur people's practice of Islam would be inconsistent with the principles outlined in Article 36. Discouraging or suppressing religious beliefs and practices would undermine the constitutional protection of religious freedom in China.
Have many well-respected Uyghur people said that the Chinese government is trying to discourage them from Islamic cultural practices?

Yes, there have been numerous reports and statements from well-respected Uyghur individuals, human rights organizations, and international observers alleging that the Chinese government has been engaging in efforts to discourage and suppress Islamic cultural practices among the Uyghur population. These reports include accounts of forced assimilation, religious restrictions, arbitrary detainment, and cultural suppression in the Xinjiang region, where a significant Uyghur population resides. While it is important to examine a variety of sources and perspectives, the allegations and concerns raised by Uyghur people and human rights advocates cannot be dismissed and require careful attention and investigation.
What are the likely consequences that Chinese government officials would face if they were found to be attempting to discourage Uyghur people from Islamic cultural practices?

The likely consequences for Chinese government officials found to be involved in attempting to discourage Uyghur people from Islamic cultural practices would depend on various factors, including the extent of their involvement, the evidence presented, and the response of domestic and international stakeholders. If such actions are determined to be a violation of human rights or constitutional guarantees, potential consequences could include:

1. Domestic Legal Action: Concerned individuals or organizations may initiate legal proceedings within China's domestic legal system seeking redress for human rights violations. However, it is important to note that the Chinese legal system is controlled by the government, and pursuing legal action against government officials may face obstacles.

2. International Pressure: The international community, including human rights organizations and foreign governments, can apply diplomatic and economic pressure on China to halt such practices. This may involve issuing public statements, imposing sanctions, or raising the issue in international forums like the United Nations.

3. Human Rights Inquiries and Investigations: International bodies and organizations dedicated to monitoring human rights violations, such as the United Nations Human Rights Council, may conduct inquiries or investigations into the situation in order to hold responsible individuals accountable.

4. Travel Restrictions and Asset Freezing: In severe cases, targeted sanctions by foreign governments could include travel restrictions and asset freezes on officials involved in human rights abuses, limiting their ability to travel and access foreign financial systems.

5. Criminal Prosecution: In certain circumstances, if evidence of gross human rights abuses emerges, government officials involved could face international criminal prosecution before tribunals like the International Criminal Court or other international mechanisms.

It's important to note that the actual consequences and outcomes depend on the actions of various actors, the complexity of the geopolitical landscape, and the respective responses by the Chinese government and the international community.

In light of the "obstacles" you mention, would it generally be considered safe for a Uyghur person to pursue domestic legal action against a government official for attempting to discourage Uyghur people from Islamic cultural practices?

Pursuing domestic legal action against a government official for attempting to discourage Uyghur people from Islamic cultural practices may carry significant risks and challenges, given the current context and restrictions faced by Uyghur individuals in China. While China has a legal system in place, it is important to consider that it operates
